title: Unbounded Knapsack
hints: This is an unbounded knapsack problem where each item can be used multiple times.
Use dynamic programming: dp[w] represents the maximum value achievable with capacity w.
For each item, iterate forward through capacities from C[i] to A (unlike 0-1 knapsack).
Iterating forward allows us to use the same item multiple times in the same iteration.
For each capacity w, decide whether to include the current item: dp[w] = max(dp[w], dp[w - C[i]] + B[i]).
The answer will be dp[A], the maximum value achievable with full capacity.
